req.title: Procedure record (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-086 mandates three orthogonal concerns: (1) a Procedure resource is persisted when a valid open Encounter exists, (2) a 422 is returned when the Encounter reference is absent or invalid, and (3) an AuditEvent is emitted for every operation. Test design covers: EP for the happy-path partition (valid encounter → 201 + AuditEvent), NEG for the missing-encounter error pathway (422), BVA for the boundary where the Encounter reference field is present but contains an empty string (degenerate boundary between 'provided' and 'missing'), STC for the state transition open-Encounter → Procedure-recorded → audit-trail-complete, and DT for the combined decision of encounter-present × subject-present to confirm the full success path. This yields five tests, exceeding the minimum of three.

tests.name: test_create_procedure_valid_encounter_returns_201_and_audit_event
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A valid open Encounter resource exists with known encounterID
Clinician auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
actions: POST /Procedure with resourceType=Procedure, status=completed, subject reference, and encounter reference pointing to the existing Encounter
Capture the response status and body
GET /AuditEvent?entity=Procedure/<new_id> to retrieve emitted audit record
expected: Response body encounter.reference matches submitted encounter ID
GET /AuditEvent returns at least one AuditEvent record whose entity reference matches the new Procedure ID
trace: R-HLT-086: procedure stored when encounter open
R-HLT-086: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_procedure_valid_encounter_returns_201_and_audit_event(
    base_url, auth_headers, open_encounter_id
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}"},
        "code": {"text": "Blood pressure measurement"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    proc_id = body.get("id")
    assert proc_id, "Response body missing 'id'"
    assert body["encounter"]["reference"] == f"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Procedure/{proc_id}"},
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "No AuditEvent emitted for Procedure creation"

tests.name: test_create_procedure_missing_encounter_returns_422
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
No encounter reference is included in the request body
Clinician auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
actions: POST /Procedure with resourceType=Procedure and status=completed but omit the encounter field entirely
Capture the response status and OperationOutcome body
expected: OperationOutcome issue severity is 'error' and diagnostics mention encounter
trace: R-HLT-086: error pathway 422 when encounter missing
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_procedure_missing_encounter_returns_422(
    base_url, auth_headers
):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "code": {"text": "Blood pressure measurement"}
        # 'encounter' field deliberately omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(
        i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues
    ), "Expected at least one error-severity issue in OperationOutcome"

tests.name: test_create_procedure_empty_string_encounter_reference_is_rejected
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
The encounter field is present in the payload but its reference value is an empty string (boundary between supplied and missing)
Clinician auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
actions: POST /Procedure with encounter field set to {"reference": ""} (empty string)
expected: POST /Procedure returns HTTP 422 or HTTP 400 (invalid reference boundary)
Procedure is NOT persisted (no id returned)
trace: R-HLT-086: error pathway when encounter reference degenerate/empty
BVA: boundary between present-encounter and absent-encounter partitions

tests.name: test_procedure_state_transition_open_encounter_to_recorded_with_audit_trail
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
A valid Encounter in status=in-progress exists
Clinician auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
actions: GET /Encounter/<id> to confirm state is in-progress (pre-state)
POST /Procedure referencing that Encounter to trigger state transition
GET /Procedure/<new_id> to confirm procedure state is completed (post-state)
GET /AuditEvent?entity=Procedure/<new_id> to verify audit trail covers the transition
expected: AuditEvent entry exists with action=C (create) for the Procedure
trace: R-HLT-086: state transition open-Encounter → Procedure recorded
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_procedure_state_transition_open_encounter_to_recorded_with_audit_trail(
    base_url, auth_headers, open_encounter_id
):
    enc_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Encounter/{open_encounter_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert enc_resp.status_code == 200
    assert enc_resp.json().get("status") == "in-progress"

    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}"},
        "code": {"text": "ECG"}
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Procedure", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    proc_id = post_resp.json()["id"]

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Procedure/{proc_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "completed"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Procedure/{proc_id}", "action": "C"},
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1

tests.name: test_procedure_decision_table_encounter_present_and_subject_present_succeeds
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Encounter exists and is open; Subject (Patient) exists
Decision table row: encounter=PRESENT, subject=PRESENT → expected outcome=201+AuditEvent
actions: POST /Procedure with both encounter reference and subject reference populated
Assert 201 and stored resource contains both references
expected: Stored Procedure contains encounter.reference and subject.reference
AuditEvent entry count >= 1 for this Procedure
trace: R-HLT-086: decision table – encounter present AND subject present → procedure stored
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_procedure_decision_table_encounter_present_and_subject_present_succeeds(
    base_url, auth_headers, open_encounter_id
):
    subject_ref = "Patient/test-patient-dt-1"
    encounter_ref = f"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}"
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": subject_ref},
        "encounter": {"reference": encounter_ref},
        "code": {"text": "Venepuncture"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Procedure", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"DT row failed: {resp.status_code} {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["subject"]["reference"] == subject_ref
    assert body["encounter"]["reference"] == encounter_ref
    proc_id = body["id"]

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Procedure/{proc_id}"},
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1, "AuditEvent missing for DT positive row"
